preferences.label: Traceability is the product
preferences.description: Every piece of output traces back to its source — page number, scan coordinates, OCR engine, confidence score, processing step. Data without provenance is noise.

preferences.label: Fidelity to the source
preferences.description: The system preserves what the document contains, not what it thinks the document means. OCR errors are bugs. Formatting changes are bugs. Missing content is a catastrophic failure.

preferences.label: Any format, any condition
preferences.description: Scanned PDFs, born-digital, images, mixed layouts, degraded quality. No format is unsupported. The system adapts to the input, not the other way around.

preferences.label: Zero configuration
preferences.description: The user's job is to provide a document. Everything else — format detection, pipeline selection, quality validation — is the system's job.

preferences.label: Dossier-ready output
preferences.description: The output format serves Dossier's ingestion requirements. Structure, provenance, and metadata are first-class. The converter's job ends when Dossier can consume the output without transformation.

preferences.label: Transparency over magic
preferences.description: When something goes wrong, the system shows you exactly what happened: which page, which module, which decision, what confidence.

preferences.label: Graduate, don't accumulate
preferences.description: Converters that are proven and stable should move to Dossier. Doc-forge stays lean — it's always working on the next unsolved problem, not maintaining solved ones.

requirements.label: Ingest
requirements.description: Accept documents in any common format (PDF, images, text, office documents) and produce a normalized internal representation.

requirements.label: Detect
requirements.description: Identify what the document is and what conversion strategy it needs. Format, quality, layout complexity, content type — all assessed automatically.

requirements.label: Extract
requirements.description: Produce clean, accurate text. OCR quality must match or exceed the best human transcription. Layout must be preserved where it carries meaning (tables, multi-column, structured data).

requirements.label: Illustrate
requirements.description: Detect, crop, and catalog every illustration with precision. Whitespace trimmed, captions preserved, overlapping text excluded.

requirements.label: Structure
requirements.description: Decompose the document into semantic parts with provenance. Every piece of output knows where it came from.

requirements.label: Validate
requirements.description: Prove the output is correct and Dossier-ready. Every page accounted for. Text accuracy verified. Structure validated against expectations.

requirements.label: Export
requirements.description: Produce output in Dossier's expected format with full provenance metadata.

stories.title: Finish Real Handwritten OCR on the LOC Fixture Pair
ideal_refs: Requirement #1 (Ingest), Requirement #2 (Detect), Requirement #3 (Extract), Any format, any condition, Fidelity to the source, Minimum Viable Floor
decision_refs: `docs/ideal.md`, `docs/spec.md`, `docs/methodology/state.yaml`, `docs/methodology/graph.json`, `docs/decisions/adr-002-doc-web-runtime-boundary/adr.md`, `docs/runbooks/golden-build.md`, `docs/stories/story-179-repo-owned-handwritten-notes-fixture-and-baseline-transcription.md`, `docs/stories/story-182-widen-handwritten-notes-fixture-breadth.md`, `docs/stories/story-185-widen-handwritten-proof-beyond-synthetic-pair.md`, `docs/stories/story-186-real-handwritten-fixture-proof-surface.md`, `docs/stories/story-189-improve-real-handwritten-barney-fidelity.md`, `docs/stories/story-190-fix-story-progression-and-anti-fragmentation-workflow.md`, `docs/evals/registry.yaml`, `tests/fixtures/formats/_coverage-matrix.json`, and `None found after search in docs/decisions/`, `docs/runbooks/`, `docs/scout/`, and `docs/notes/` for a narrower handwritten full-page rescue ADR or runbook
stories.blocker_summary: The current checked-in LOC handwritten proof pair cannot honestly clear the `0.99` handwritten bar in this repo state because two blockers are now explicit: Barney remains below bar across the maintained rescue seam and the fresh stronger direct-model probes, and the checked-in Alverson front-page source/transcript pair is not source-faithful enough to serve as a clean OCR target for the continuation text Story 189/191 had been treating as “missing lower-page content.”
stories.blocker_evidence:
- Fresh maintained baseline rerun on current tip wrote `benchmarks/results/handwritten-notes-story191-baseline-20260404.json` with `pass_rate = 0.6`, `overall_min_ratio = 0.499136`, and `page_min_ratio = 0.499136`. The six synthetic rescue cases still passed at `1.0`, but the real fixtures stayed below bar: Barney scored `0.895587` on image entry and `0.756036` on PDF entry, while Alverson scored `0.499136` on image entry and `0.500647` on PDF entry.
- Fresh direct stronger-model probes in this pass did not overturn that result. `gpt-5` direct runs at `output/runs/story191-barney-image-gpt5-direct/02_ocr_ai_gpt51_v1/pages_html.jsonl`, `output/runs/story191-barney-pdf-gpt5-direct/02_ocr_ai_gpt51_v1/pages_html.jsonl`, `output/runs/story191-alverson-image-gpt5-direct/02_ocr_ai_gpt51_v1/pages_html.jsonl`, and `output/runs/story191-alverson-pdf-gpt5-direct/02_ocr_ai_gpt51_v1/pages_html.jsonl` scored `0.192010`, `0.010297`, `0.221929`, and `0.362143`. `claude-opus-4-6` direct image probes at `output/runs/story191-barney-image-claude-direct/02_ocr_ai_gpt51_v1/pages_html.jsonl` and `output/runs/story191-alverson-image-claude-direct/02_ocr_ai_gpt51_v1/pages_html.jsonl` scored `0.725708` and `0.193325`.
- Manual source review changed the interpretation of the Alverson failure. The checked-in source image `testdata/handwritten-notes-alverson-real-images/page-001.jpg` visibly ends on the same line where the current OCR stops (`...exceptable and`). The current checked-in asset also matches the LOC front-page large JPEG dimensions (`638x1024`). Meanwhile the LOC source record for item `2023637835` presents separate `Front` and `Back` images, so the continuation text that the transcript carries after that boundary is not recoverable from the checked-in front-page image alone. This means the current Alverson truth surface is at least partially a source/transcript alignment problem, not just an OCR runtime problem.
- Additional one-off local window and enhancement probes in this pass did not reveal a bounded maintained rescue shape that could rescue the lane honestly. Cropped-window rereads and contrast/upscale variants still failed to recover the missing Alverson continuation from the front-page image and did not demonstrate a reliable Barney improvement strong enough to justify landing new runtime logic.
stories.unblock_condition:
Unblock this story only when both halves are true:

1. The Alverson truth surface is corrected so the checked-in source assets and transcript actually match the intended OCR target, for example by trimming the transcript to the visible front-page content or by replacing the fixture with a source-faithful front/back pair plus page-separated transcript.
2. A materially different OCR substrate then clears the real pair, not just the synthetic fixtures. That can be a new model, a better source asset, or a genuinely stronger recovery seam, but it must produce fresh current-pass `driver.py` artifacts that bring Barney and the corrected Alverson fixture to `overall_min_ratio >= 0.99`, `page_min_ratio >= 0.99`, and `pass_rate = 1.0`.

evals.name: OCR Model Eval — Genealogy / Table-Heavy Books
C2 Detection: Auto Book Type Detection
C3 Proof: Maintained Layout Section Splitting

formats.description: Scanned PDF with primarily prose content

fixtures.notes: Repo-owned image-only prose PDF generated from checked-in source text. Story 167 adds maintained extractor-provenance smoke plus a fresh OCR proof whose normalized output matches the checked-in source text exactly.

scores.notes: Measured against the checked-in `scanned-prose-mini.md` source after Story 167's fresh `driver.py` proof. This is a clean repo-owned simple-prose scanned lane, not a proxy for degraded or noisy scans.

known_gaps: Current evidence is a clean synthetic scanned fixture only; broader noisy real-world scanned-prose quality remains unmeasured.

formats.description: Scanned PDF with dense tables (genealogy, reference books)

fixtures.notes: Shared local scanned PDF for the maintained Onward table-heavy lane. Matches the 127-page shared image-directory source.

scores.notes: Structure quality comes from Story 131 / the 2026-03-11 onward-table-fidelity refresh, illustration quality uses the 2026-03-11 crop eval (0.900), and Story 157 restored maintained PDF-backed entry plus fresh provenance verification on the shared Onward PDF.

known_gaps: Handwritten annotations still weaken some table pages.
Illustration extraction remains below the 0.95 graduation target.

fixtures.notes: Same book as scanned-pdf-tables but ingested as pre-extracted images.

scores.notes: Shares the current scanned-image quality envelope: table structure at 0.95, illustration extraction at 0.900, and provenance completeness at 1.0 after Story 132 verification.

known_gaps: Illustration extraction remains below the 0.95 graduation target.

scores.notes: Text passthrough — no OCR needed. Structure preservation N/A for plain text.
Markdown passthrough. Structure (headers, lists) preserved as-is.

known_gaps: No semantic structure extraction from markdown headers/lists
No tag stripping or semantic extraction from HTML structure

formats.description: PDFs with embedded text (no OCR needed), possibly with complex layouts

fixtures.notes: Repo-owned reviewed book-like fixture. Story 168 proved the bounded maintained Marker-lite native-text lane here, and Story 170 reverified it against the OCR baseline.

Repo-owned reproducible flat born-digital fixture generated from checked-in source text. Story 171 proved the maintained non-TOC lane here with accepted `page_html_v1`, portion, bundle, and provenance artifacts.

Repo-owned reproducible flat born-digital form-like fixture generated from checked-in source text. Story 177 uses it to widen the repeatable proof surface beyond the original prose-only mini fixture and to verify bounded heading polish on short label-style sections.

Story 170 first exposed this short flat born-digital PDF as a downstream structure failure. Story 171 reran it through the maintained non-TOC recipe and produced an accepted single-document bundle with page-linked provenance.

Story 170 first exposed this form-heavy born-digital PDF as a downstream structure failure. Story 171 reran it through the maintained non-TOC recipe and produced an accepted single-document bundle with page-linked provenance.

known_gaps: Support still rests on a small proof surface: two repo-owned flat fixtures plus two shared local flat PDFs. This is enough for `has-fixture`, not for broad passing/graduation claims.

Story 177 absorbs bounded final-HTML heading polish on the proven slice: repeated short in-body h1/h2 labels are demoted and pathological long heading blocks are flattened into emphasis paragraphs, but `page_html_v1` still reflects raw Marker heading guesses before build-time cleanup.
Image-heavy or otherwise unproven flat born-digital PDFs can still honestly return `no-recipe-needed` when intake evidence does not justify the non-TOC lane.
Marker remains materially slower on cold start than the OCR baseline.

fixtures.notes: Repo-owned DOCX fixture generated from checked-in source JSON. Story 172 proved the first maintained lane; Story 175 re-verified it under the widened three-fixture proof surface.
Repo-owned DOCX fixture with simple multi-section prose that previously surfaced false chapter splits when Unstructured promoted sentence-like body text to top-level `Title`.

Repo-owned DOCX fixture with nested subheadings plus bullets. Story 175 used it to prove the widened maintained slice can keep nested heading content inside the parent chapter rather than inventing extra chapters.

known_gaps: Passing evidence is still bounded to the supported slice reviewed in Story 175: heading-based sections, prose, nested subheadings, simple bullet lists, and a simple table.
The maintained lane does not yet claim tracked changes, comments, text boxes, embedded charts, or other advanced Word features.
Pageless DOCX provenance currently relies on stable source element ids rather than page-linked open-original behavior.

fixtures.notes: Repo-owned XLSX workbook generated from checked-in source JSON. Story 175 used it to prove the first maintained XLSX slice: simple table sheets become one HTML page per sheet with anchor-based provenance.

known_gaps: Current proof is only one repo-owned workbook on the narrow simple-table slice.
The maintained lane does not yet claim formulas, charts, merged cells, images, comments, or cell-level open-original behavior.
Workbook provenance currently relies on stable source element ids and sheet-named entries rather than cell-address anchors.

fixtures.notes: Reproducible PPTX seam-probe fixture generated from checked-in markdown via `pandoc`. Story 175 used it to make the current PPTX runtime block explicit.

known_gaps: The local PPTX seam is still blocked in this checkout: `unstructured.partition.pptx` fails on missing `python-pptx`.
No maintained PPTX recipe or provenance mapping exists yet.
Slide reading order, mixed-layout ownership, speaker notes, and embedded media remain unproven.

formats.description: Email messages (.eml) with headers, body, and attachments
Photos of handwritten notes, letters, diaries

fixtures.notes: Repo-owned synthetic handwritten note image directory generated from checked-in transcript text. Story 179 proved the maintained generic image-directory OCR lane on this narrow highly legible slice.

Image-only PDF wrapper around the same synthetic handwritten pages. Story 179 verified `pypdf` extract-text lengths `[0, 0]` and matched the checked-in transcript exactly after normalization on the maintained generic PDF OCR lane.

Repo-owned faded synthetic handwritten note image directory generated from a checked-in transcript via `generate_handwritten_notes_fixture.py --preset faded`. Story 185 widened the maintained generic image-directory OCR lane to a third synthetic handwritten slice with smaller, lighter, more jittered text.

Image-only PDF wrapper around the faded synthetic handwritten pages. Story 185 verified `pypdf` extract-text lengths `[0, 0]` and matched the checked-in transcript exactly after normalization on the maintained generic PDF OCR lane.

Repo-owned rougher synthetic handwritten note image directory generated from a checked-in transcript via `generate_handwritten_notes_fixture.py --preset rough`. Story 182 widened the maintained generic image-directory OCR lane beyond the original legible slice while keeping the support claim explicitly synthetic-only.

Image-only PDF wrapper around the rough synthetic handwritten pages. Story 182 verified `pypdf` extract-text lengths `[0, 0, 0]` and matched the checked-in transcript exactly after normalization on the maintained generic PDF OCR lane.

Repo-owned real handwritten fixture derived from Library of Congress item `https://www.loc.gov/pictures/item/2023637825/`, using the checked-in `LC-DIG-ppmsca-86618` pages 2-3 spread. Story 186 proved maintained image-directory provenance on this real slice, but the generic OCR lane stayed only in the low-0.1 range on reruns. Story 189 added a bounded handwritten rescue recipe; the fresh Story 191 maintained-baseline rerun still only reached `0.895587` on image entry, and stronger direct-model probes in the same pass still stayed below bar, so Barney remains a real-slice OCR failure even after the rescue seam.

Image-only PDF wrapper around the same real LOC Barney spread. Story 186 verified `pypdf` extract-text lengths `[0]` and reran the maintained generic PDF OCR lane, which also stayed only in the low-0.1 range on the checked-in transcript excerpt. Story 189 added a bounded handwritten rescue recipe, but the fresh Story 191 maintained-baseline rerun only reached `0.756036` on PDF entry, which is still well below bar.

Second repo-owned real handwritten fixture derived from Library of Congress item `https://www.loc.gov/pictures/item/2023637835/`, using the checked-in front page (`LC-DIG-cwpb-08756`). Story 189 widened the real handwritten proof surface with this page; the fresh Story 191 maintained-baseline rerun only reached `0.499136` on image entry. Story 191 then verified a truth-surface blocker: the checked-in front-page image visibly ends at the same `...exceptable and` boundary where OCR stops, while the LOC source record exposes separate front/back images, so the current transcript appears to extend beyond the visible front-page source.

Image-only PDF wrapper around the same real LOC Alverson front page. Story 189 verified `pypdf` extract-text lengths `[0]`; the fresh Story 191 maintained-baseline rerun reached only `0.500647` on PDF entry. Story 191 also confirmed that the continuation text missing from the current OCR output is not present in the checked-in front-page source asset, so this fixture is blocked by source/transcript misalignment as well as OCR quality.

known_gaps: The three repo-owned synthetic handwritten fixtures still pass exactly after normalization across six maintained handwritten-rescue image-directory and PDF OCR cases, but the fresh Story 191 five-fixture corpus rerun still falls to `pass_rate = 0.6` because Barney misses on both entry seams (`0.895587` image / `0.756036` pdf) and the checked-in Alverson front-page fixture remains below bar (`0.499136` image / `0.500647` pdf).

The bounded handwritten Gemini rescue seam is materially better than the historical generic low-0.1 Barney baseline, but it is not stable or general enough: Barney still drifts on names and words such as `Harlen`, `genesee`, and `father has had gone`, and fresh current-pass `gpt-5` / `claude-opus-4-6` direct probes also stayed below bar.

Story 191 found a truth-surface blocker on Alverson: the checked-in front-page image itself ends at the same `...exceptable and` boundary where OCR stops, while the current transcript continues beyond that visible source and the LOC source record exposes separate front/back images. The handwritten lane therefore remains blocked on both OCR capability and source/transcript alignment.
Messy cursive, degraded notes, and broader real-world handwriting collections remain unproven.

formats.description: ZIP or folder containing multiple file types that need format detection and routing

statuses.untested: Format identified but no test fixtures or pipeline support
statuses.has-fixture: Test document exists but pipeline not yet built or passing
statuses.passing: Pipeline converts this format with acceptable accuracy
statuses.graduated: Converter migrated to Dossier — doc-forge no longer the primary owner

coverage_matrix.notes: Canonical machine-readable input-coverage surface. The methodology compiler reads this directly instead of duplicating its rows inside state.yaml.

sequencing_bias.reason: Keep the active intake and doc-web path honest before widening new format promises.
Replace the live hand-authored build-map/index surfaces with structured state, compiled graph, and generated views.

campaigns.notes: Keep format claims tied to current fixture/eval evidence instead of widening through prose drift.
Migrate methodology consumers onto state + graph + coverage-matrix truth and retire the live hand-authored build map.

sections.title: Recommended Order
sections.markdown:
Guiding priorities: keep the active intake + `doc-web` path honest, improve validation and provenance, expand reusable format coverage, and retire or quarantine legacy FF/gamebook surfaces that no longer belong in the default roadmap.

Use `docs/methodology/graph.json`, `docs/methodology/state.yaml`, the active `doc-web` stories, and `/triage` output for current sequencing. Historical Fighting Fantasy/gamebook stories remain in the index below as project record and reference evidence, not as the default mission queue.

methodology_tooling.label: Methodology Tooling
methodology_tooling.notes: Methodology graph/state migration and legacy metadata backfill are the active audit lane.

intake_and_routing.label: Intake and Routing
intake_and_routing.notes: Maintained intake handoff and routing honesty.

ocr_and_extraction.label: OCR and Extraction
ocr_and_extraction.notes: Recent handwritten-fixture work widened evidence but did not trigger a structural cleanup story yet.

document_structure_and_consistency.label: Document Structure and Consistency
document_structure_and_consistency.notes: Layout benchmark and crop/runtime simplification recently moved; next audit should look for deletable residue.

doc_web_runtime.label: doc-web Runtime
doc_web_runtime.notes: Stable runtime boundary with follow-up width still mostly in fixture/coverage work.
